1._: Sable sits at the far end of the long trestle table with a piece of scraped hide in front of her on which she has already copied the marks she saw on the gather-stone from memory, her chalk moving in short certain strokes. She is aware of Maret Coll near the door and has returned the hall-keeper's held gaze with a nod that answered nothing. The maps weighted down by stones and cups are of interest to her — she has glanced at them twice — but she has not yet moved to look more closely.

content._: sable's account at Thousand Valleys, beat by beat.
